title: Region-Based Segmentation Flow
description: A flowchart illustrating the combined process of region growing, splitting, and merging to segment an image into coherent regions.
relationships: Process starts with the Original Image, which is used to define Initial Seed Points
Region Growing Process uses seeds and a homogeneity criterion to expand regions
If a region fails the homogeneity check, it is passed to Region Splitting
Region Splitting divides a heterogeneous region into smaller sub-regions (e.g., quadrants)
Resulting regions from growing and splitting are evaluated for merging
Region Merging combines adjacent regions that are similar according to the criterion
The process iterates until no more splits or merges are needed, resulting in the Final Segmented Image
